div.p: Every layer requires an all-gather to fetch parameters from DP shards

g.text: Discard: Free [W₁, W₂, W₃] on Rank 0
Stage | Param Comm | Grad Comm | Optim Comm

div.p: Key Trade-off: ZeRO-3 saves 8× parameter memory but adds communication to every layer's forward pass. Use ZeRO-3 when:

ul.li: Communication bandwidth is high (NVLink) Avoid ZeRO-3 when:

text.tspan: # 1. All-gather parameters from all DP ranks
All-gather parameter shards from DP ranks.
# 1. All-gather parameters from all DP ranks
All-gather parameter shards from DP ranks.

div.p: Each DP rank broadcasts its parameter shard. Communication volume: param_size / DP_size per rank. Total data movement: param_size × (DP_size - 1) / DP_size
Y = Layer(X, W_full) Full parameter matrix available for matmul. Standard compute, no communication.
Free memory for non-owned parameter shards. Only keep locally owned W_local. Memory freed: param_size × (DP_size - 1) / DP_size
Large layers (e.g., FFN 4× hidden dim) have:

ul.li: High compute: O(batch × seq × hidden × ffn_dim)
High comm: O(hidden × ffn_dim / DP) Comm/Compute ratio = (ffn_dim / DP) / (batch × seq × hidden) Small batches → comm dominates!
Fixed latency overhead per all-gather For 80 layers: 480+ all-gathers Latency overhead: α × 480 × log(DP)

ol.li: Overlap comm/compute: Gather next layer while computing current
Gradient checkpointing: Reduces activation memory, allows larger batch
Mixed ZeRO: Use ZeRO-3 for large layers, ZeRO-2 for small
